Localization.Description: Password spraying via SSH. Password spraying is a type of brute-force attack in which an attacker tries the most popular passwords sequentially for each user, which helps avoid blocking the accounts of the attacked users.

EventDescriptions.EventDescription: Password spraying attempts via SSH from host {src.host} on host {event_src.host}. Number of login attempts: {datafield1}.
Successful password spraying via SSH from host {src.host} on host {event_src.host}. Attackers obtained the password of user {subject.account.name}. Number of failed login attempts: {datafield1}.

Successful password spraying via SSH from host {src.host} on host {event_src.host}. Attackers obtained the passwords of the following users: {datafield2}. Number of failed login attempts: {datafield1}.
